name: crra_haircut_u_transform_contract
purpose: Pins the _haircut_select CRRA-EU branch contract: daily_returns (raw percent) must be U-transformed before being passed to compute_crra_eu_tstat. Provides two scenarios: (1) a contract scenario with a tail-heavy return distribution for the CRRA-001 characterization test; (2) a divergence scenario with extreme losses for the Sharpe-vs-CRRA divergence test. Both scenarios demonstrate that t_from_raw_pct materially differs from t_crra_eu.
provenance: Non-circular: all values derived by closed-form arithmetic from the CRRA utility formula U(W;gamma) = (W^(1-gamma)-1)/(1-gamma) with W = max(WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR, 1 + r/RETURN_PCT_TO_FRACTION). No autotuner or SUT output was used. Recipe verifiable by evaluating the closed-form expressions at the given r_pct values.

constants.crra_formula: U(W; gamma) = (W^(1-gamma) - 1) / (1-gamma) for gamma != 1; ln(W) for gamma==1

scenario_contract.description: Tail-heavy distribution: mixed large losses and moderate gains. No WEALTH_ARG_FLOOR hit. Chosen so t_crra_eu and t_from_raw_pct differ by ~52% relative, ensuring that a broken implementation (passing raw percent to compute_crra_eu_tstat) cannot accidentally produce the correct value.

expected.discriminating_note: t_crra_eu and t_from_raw_pct differ by ~52% relative. A broken implementation that passes raw percent values to compute_crra_eu_tstat would return t_from_raw_pct (-0.538), not t_crra_eu (-1.131). The two values cannot be within rel=0.05 of each other.

scenario_divergence.description: Ranking-divergence scenario: two competing trial distributions where raw-percent ranking and CRRA-EU ranking materially differ. Trial A has one extreme loss + moderate gains; Trial B has uniform moderate losses. CRRA penalizes the extreme loss in Trial A more than the Sharpe-like form does, leading to a larger divergence in t-stats.

expected.note: t_crra is ~83% more negative than t_raw; extreme CRRA penalty from W=0.05 on day 0.

ranking_check.note: Rankings happen to agree for this pair, but the t-stat magnitudes differ materially (trial A: crra=-0.977 vs raw=-0.534). The divergence test asserts the returned value matches the crra form, not the raw form.

tolerance_notes.contract_test: rel=1e-9 on t_crra_eu — deterministic double-precision arithmetic on fixed inputs; only last-bit rounding allowed.
tolerance_notes.divergence_test: rel=0.05 separator — the two t-stat values differ by >50% relative; a broken implementation returning t_from_raw_pct cannot be within 5% of t_crra_eu.
